task.title: Preserve file mode bits when rewriting formatted files
task.summary: Running the formatter through the current rewrite path drops executable bits from script fixtures even when file contents do not change meaningfully.
task.problem: The fixture review showed fixtures/dotfiles/scripts/update.nu changing mode from 100755 to 100644. This is a tooling side effect, not a formatting judgment, and it can break workflows that rely on executable Nushell scripts.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Formatting and rewriting a file preserves its executable bit and any other relevant mode bits.
A regression test or fixture-based verification demonstrates that a 100755 `.nu` file remains executable after formatting.
The fix applies to the supported formatter invocation paths, not just a one-off local script.
